hero.question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 7.2%, the market is pricing Steyer as a longshot with almost no real path to the governorship, which feels about right for a guy who already spent $200M+ of his own money losing statewide races. The real question is whether he runs at all in 2026, and if he does, whether name recognition and self-funding juice him above 15% in a crowded field before reality sets in.

daily_take.headline: Steyer's California Comeback Bet Just Got Real Cheap
daily_take.deck: Tom Steyer's 2026 gubernatorial odds jumped 4.2 points to 7.2% on $886K volume, signaling fresh momentum behind the billionaire's second run. The move smells like smart money repositioning before the field hardens. At 7.2%, you're getting a wealthy self-funder with name recognition at a fraction of what a serious candidate typically prices. If Newsom fatigue or a left-wing opening emerges by 2025, this is the kind of long shot that punches above its odds.
